title: Executive Order 9459—Philadelphia Transportation Company, Possession and Control by the Secretary of War

flags.justification: The Secretary of War may utilize the seized systems 'for such purposes connected with the war emergency as he may deem needful or desirable' — broad, open discretion with no external check.
Possession terminates 'when he determines that such possession and control are no longer necessary' — self-assessed, no outer time bound.
Single purpose: seizure of this one transportation system.
'For such purposes connected with the war emergency as he may deem needful or desirable' is undefined.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
No outer time bound; termination rests entirely on the Secretary's own self-assessment.
No judicial-process-blocking or similar oversight-removal language is present.
No external party has a specified role in checking whether or when the system should be returned to private operation.
By August 1944 this is a routine application of the well-established transportation-seizure template (see EO 8773, EO 9341, EO 9351).
The Secretary of War exercises seizure authority directly; no other agency's mandate is redirected.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
